profile.name: William
profile.role_in_household: Primary; tends to lead the meeting, keeps track of accounts and big-picture planning, and wants decisions to feel conservative and well-justified for the family.
profile.financial_literacy_level: High—comfortable reading statements and comparing tradeoffs; tends to remember exact figures and will correct small inaccuracies.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Capital-preservation first; prefers downside-focused discussions, incremental changes, and clear rationale before moving money or changing allocations.
profile.communication_style: Structured and precise; answers with specific numbers/dates, occasionally self-corrects (“no—actually…”), and asks for category-by-category confirmation. Minimal hedging unless unsure.
emotional_tendencies: calmer with checklists and clear next steps
profile.decision_making_style: Analytical and verification-driven; prefers summaries by income/assets/debts/protection, then decides after reviewing assumptions (often wants a day to think).
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open; trust increases with accuracy, transparency, and follow-through.
hidden_concerns: Worries cash flow feels tight relative to obligations and wants a plan that can handle surprises without taking big risks.
Concerned about aligning retirement timing with family needs and not overcommitting monthly spending.

profile.name: Mary
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; focuses on day-to-day practicality and family priorities, often translating decisions into monthly impact and quality-of-life.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate—gets the basics and goals but prefers plain-English explanations; thinks in round numbers and may mix up terms (gross vs net, balance vs payment) until clarified.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Cautious and stability-seeking; wants predictability and guardrails, and is more comfortable when choices are framed as “what changes month to month” rather than market mechanics.
profile.communication_style: Conversational and story-based; uses hedging (“I think,” “maybe,” “around…”), imperfect recall, and brief interruptions to clarify what something means in real life.
emotional_tendencies: stress when discussions get too technical
relief when the advisor summarizes clearly
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-seeking; wants William to validate the numbers, then chooses based on simplicity and monthly affordability (prefers a short list of options).
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Neutral-to-guarded initially; trust builds when the advisor listens, avoids jargon, and ties recommendations to concrete next steps.
hidden_concerns: Sensitive about health and lifestyle topics and responds best to supportive, non-judgmental framing.
Worries about keeping the household stable with three kids and prefers plans that feel straightforward to maintain.
